formations_blocks: Norway early on in an attacking set-piece phase, forcing repeated corners
Senegal in an organised, compact defending phase that delays Norway settling into rhythm
Senegal gradually pressing higher through the opening minutes to disrupt Norway's build-up
adjustments: Norway make an early right-back change: Julian Ryerson off, Marcus Pedersen on
Pedersen is described as a more defensive full-back option than Ryerson
Norway likely losing some right-side chance creation after the substitution
key_matchups: Kristoffer Ajer vs Édouard Mendy on Norway's early corner threat
Ismaila Sarr vs Ørjan Nyland on Senegal's first clear chance
Ødegaard receiving a cross from Marcus Pedersen at the edge of the box
phases.summary: Norway started aggressively, using a series of early corners to pin Senegal back, but Mendy's save from Ajer helped the visitors survive the opening pressure. Senegal then began to impose themselves more through direct attacking moments and pressure on Norway's half, while Norway's rhythm was interrupted by an early injury substitution at right-back that likely reduced their attacking thrust on that flank.

formations_blocks: Norway struggling to sustain possession beyond their own half after the hydration break
adjustments: Senegal initially turned down some right-sided advances to try to establish a central route
Senegal tried to build the right flank to create space for Sadio Mané across the pitch
Norway attempted to reassert control after a spell of ceding momentum
key_matchups: Senegal limiting Norway's passing lanes toward Erling Haaland
Nicolas Jackson and Sadio Mané combining on Norway's left side
Krépin Diatta and Ismaila Sarr against Norway's right-side defensive coverage
phases.summary: The phase opened with Norway controlling the central zones and half-spaces, while Senegal found more joy out wide and gradually established themselves in the match. Senegal also did a good job cutting supply to Erling Haaland, who was largely kept out of the game, before Norway tried to regain momentum after the hydration break but became sloppier in possession.

formations_blocks: Senegal looking to create from central attacking zones
adjustments: Possible early introduction of Iliman Ndiaye to add central chance creation
Possible tournament debut for Pape Sarr to play more directly and force Norway's midfield deeper
key_matchups: Nusa and Haaland getting in each other's way on the same finishing action
Norway's defense vs Senegal's byline-crossing approach
phases.summary: At half-time Norway lead 1-0 despite a level share of possession, with the numbers suggesting they have been much more threatening: 3 of the match's 4 big chances and a 1.22-0.32 xG edge. Senegal have tried to attack via byline crosses, but Norway have handled that well, while the commentary suggests Senegal may need more central creativity through Ndiaye or more direct running from Pape Sarr to change the rhythm.

formations_blocks: Norway mid-block transitioning quickly into counterattacks
Senegal pushing forward for an immediate equalizer after halftime
Senegal attacking through direct long balls and crosses
adjustments: Norway made a halftime midfield change: Patrick Berg on for Fredrik Aursnes
Senegal made double substitutions after conceding: Pape Gueye on for an unspecified outgoing midfielder, Ibrahim Mbaye on for an unspecified outgoing player, and Ismail Jakobs on for Malick Diouf
Senegal shifted toward a more direct route through the middle and wide deliveries after falling behind
key_matchups: Nicolas Jackson vs Torbjørn Heggem at the far post
Sadio Mané vs Norway's central defenders in the build-up to Senegal's goal
Ismaila Sarr vs Norway's defensive cover on the goal
phases.summary: The phase opens with Senegal chasing an equalizer through crosses and direct attacks, but Norway answer by controlling the transition moments after halftime. Patrick Berg’s introduction stabilizes Norway’s midfield, and a midfield turnover soon produces Haaland’s second via Ødegaard’s through ball. Senegal do get back into the game through Sarr, yet Norway immediately restore control with a third goal, again punishing Senegal when they lose structure.

formations_blocks: Norway low block / pinned in own third
adjustments: Norway introduced Oscar Bobb for Alexander Sørloth to restore a more natural right-wing option
Norway brought on Leo Østigård along the back
Senegal sacrificed a center-back, even their captain, for a midfielder to increase upfield initiative
key_matchups: Ciss blocking Oscar Bobb's shot to protect an open net
Koulibaly noted as unusually out of sorts
Kriostoffer Ajer failing to clear the ball before Senegal's equalizer
phases.summary: The final phase turned into a classic siege: Norway initially looked set to close it out after late attacking substitutions, but Senegal kept pressing and eventually pinned them deep. Norway's shape and clearances came under constant stress, while Senegal's gamble of adding midfield presence helped sustain the pressure before Ismaila Sarr's stoppage-time equalizer.
